headlines.title: Jeremy Bowen: Trump is waging war based on instinct and it isn't working
headlines.description: One month into the conflict in Iran, Trump's gut-instinct approach is not proving effective, writes the BBC's international editor.

headlines.title: Our skin is falling off and no-one can tell us why
headlines.description: Posts with #TSW have had over a billion views on TikTok, sparking research into this mystery skin condition.

headlines.title: No Kings protesters across the US rally against Donald Trump
headlines.description: One of the flagship No Kings rallies happened in Minnesota, where singer Bruce Springsteen performed to crowds.

headlines.title: Antonelli wins to become youngest title leader
headlines.description: Kimi Antonelli takes his second win in succession and the lead of the world championship at the Japanese Grand Prix.

headlines.title: Frosting, sprinkles and layers of fun: Giant cake picnic hits Sydney
headlines.description: Hundreds of bakers head to the city's botanic gardens to share and savour their colourful creations.

headlines.title: One ant for $220: The new frontier of wildlife trafficking
headlines.description: The craze for collecting ants takes Kenya by surprise as smugglers zone in to make a profit.

headlines.title: Think Love Island is bad? Wait until you see the AI fruit version
headlines.description: Like in Love Island, the characters - or fruits - compete for a chance to couple up and stay on the island.

headlines.title: Freya Ridings on buying a plane ticket and reclaiming her career: 'I felt like a naughty schoolchild'
headlines.description: The singer defied her team and fled to LA to record a new album. It was the best move she's ever made.

headlines.title: How deepfake porn scandal surrounding TV star rocked Germany
headlines.description: Collien Fernandes has accused her ex-husband of spreading images of her online, but he has categorically denied it.

headlines.title: Potential Houthi threat to Red Sea shipping could further damage global economy
headlines.description: The Iran-backed group could bring a second crucial waterway to a standstill, writes Sebastian Usher.

headlines.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
headlines.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib from the Hezbollah-affiliated Al Manar TV.

headlines.title: Tears and defiance as Lebanese family bury child, 11, killed in Israeli strike
headlines.description: Jawad Younes and his uncle Ragheb were laid to rest on Saturday after their family compound was hit a day earlier.

headlines.title: Schools do not have enough staff to make SEND reforms work, union warns
headlines.description: The National Education Union says schools need more funding to be able to make all classrooms inclusive.

headlines.title: The Papers:  'US marines head to war' and 'We'll stop antisocial media'
headlines.description: The PM speaks to the Mirror on social media, following landmark US verdicts against Meta and YouTube.

headlines.title: Itauma knocks out Franklin cold - what next for 21-year-old rising British heavyweight?
headlines.description: Briton Moses Itauma thought 'did I really do that' after knocking out Jermaine Franklin with an uppercut in the fifth round of their fight in Manchester.

headlines.title: Prison phone call recordings raise questions over ex-Abercrombie boss' fitness for trial
headlines.description: Mike Jeffries' lawyers argue that he is suffering with dementia and late onset of Alzheimer's disease.

headlines.title: India restoring a centuries-old royal kitchen that never stopped serving food
headlines.description: The royal kitchen still serves food, but the complex has been worn down by time.

headlines.title: Another car crash and another arrest - what now for Tiger Woods?
headlines.description: BBC golf correspondent Iain Carter assesses where Tiger Woods' latest arrest leaves his legacy.

headlines.title: Four held in murder inquiry after death of young woman found in street
headlines.description: Police say a young woman was found unconscious on Kennerleigh Avenue in Leeds on Saturday.

headlines.title: The six-year-old who can name every country's flag
headlines.description: Kian, from Ipswich, is now learning the world's capital cities after mastering his flags.

headlines.title: Library book loaned in Dudley returned Down Under
headlines.description: Librarians on both sides of the world are in shock over the book's mystery journey.

headlines.title: Scottish gran breaks Hyrox fitness record after three hip replacements
headlines.description: Carole Munro, 72, from Turriff, Aberdeenshire, said fitness has kept her mobile and strong.

headlines.title: Ruins of Nine Days' Queen's home to reopen
headlines.description: Visitors will be welcomed back to the historic site while a major conservation project continues.

headlines.title: Man wins £1m using late dad's lottery numbers
headlines.description: Sean Hayes describes finding out the news in the pub, and he now plans to buy a house.

headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Why Josh Simons Resigned From Government
headlines.description: The former minister says he’d been ‘naive’ and is ‘sorry’ over journalist investigation.

headlines.title: What is RFK doing to vaccines for children in America?
headlines.description: Trump’s Health Secretary hits a major setback to “Make America Healthy Again”

headlines.title: Lively, bold animated Spider-Man adventure

headlines.title: Fear and Gibson miss out after 'incorrect' penalty

headlines.title: Were England players really 'playing for themselves' against Uruguay?

headlines.title: Fans booing Scotland surprise and disappoint Clarke

headlines.title: Can you ever stop supporting your football team?
headlines.description: Is it really a lifelong pledge of loyalty? BBC Sport asks whether a fan can really stop supporting their football team.
